description: Deep-dive performance analysis for Grafana database health, Go runtime, memory allocation, garbage collection, and process-level resource utilization. The first dashboard to check during a Grafana performance incident.

panels.description: Database connection pool utilization. >80% = danger zone.
panels.title: DB Pool Saturation

panels.description: Rate of goroutines waiting for a database connection. Non-zero = pool exhaustion.
panels.title: DB Wait Rate

panels.description: Average time goroutines spend waiting for a DB connection. >100ms means queries are visibly delayed.
panels.title: Avg DB Wait Time

panels.description: Grafana process resident memory (RSS). Track for unexpected growth indicating memory leaks.
panels.title: Memory Usage

panels.description: GC pause time in ms/sec. High values mean Go is spending too much time in garbage collection.
panels.title: GC Pause Rate

panels.description: File descriptor utilization. >80% = risk of 'too many open files' errors crashing Grafana.
panels.title: FD Saturation

panels.description: Complete view of the database connection pool. When 'In Use' approaches 'Max Open', new requests will be queued.
panels.title: Connection Pool State

panels.description: Connection pool lifecycle. Frequent closures indicate pool thrashing. Waits indicate saturation.
panels.title: Connection Lifecycle Events

panels.description: Total time spent waiting for DB connections per second. This is direct user-visible latency.
panels.title: Connection Wait Duration

panels.description: Correlation between database wait time and HTTP latency. If they move together, the DB is the bottleneck.
panels.title: Wait Correlation: DB Wait vs HTTP Latency

panels.description: Go heap memory states. Large gap between Sys and In Use indicates fragmentation.
panels.title: Heap Memory

panels.description: Memory allocation throughput. High allocation rates drive GC pressure.
panels.title: Memory Allocation Rate

panels.description: Non-heap memory usage. Stack growth correlates with goroutine count.
panels.title: Stack & Other Memory

panels.description: GC cycle frequency. Higher frequency = more memory pressure.
panels.title: GC Cycles

panels.description: Individual GC pause durations. Max pause directly impacts tail latency.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration

panels.description: What percentage of CPU time is spent in garbage collection. >10% indicates significant GC overhead.
panels.title: GC Overhead %

panels.description: Process CPU utilization. 100% = 1 full core.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: Active goroutines. Sustained increase without plateau = goroutine leak.
panels.title: Goroutines

panels.description: File descriptor usage vs system limit. Exhaustion causes 'too many open files' errors.
panels.title: File Descriptors

panels.description: Multi-signal correlation chart for incident analysis. When multiple signals spike together, this reveals the root cause chain: e.g., high CPU -> GC pressure -> DB saturation -> HTTP errors.
panels.title: Full Stack Correlation

title: Grafana Admin — Database & Performance Deep Dive
